findings.line: The sanctum's banality became unbearable. The tea cup still sat cold by his elbow. Ink still shone wet at the edge of an unfinished note. The chair under her hand was only chair, wood polished by years of use.
findings.reason: Four consecutive sentences open with 'The [noun]' — rhythmic inventory template.
findings.suggestedFix: Cold tea sat by his elbow. Ink still shone wet at the edge of an unfinished note. Under her hand the chair was wood, polished smooth by years of use.

findings.line: Neither of them spoke for a while. Wards cycled under stone. Water sounded somewhere beyond the wall. The lamp on Kel'Thuzad's desk had burned lower. A skin had formed over the cold tea.
findings.reason: Five consecutive subject-first declaratives describing the room — rhythmic sameness.
findings.suggestedFix: Neither of them spoke. Wards cycled under stone. Beyond the wall, water. On Kel'Thuzad's desk the lamp had burned lower, a skin forming over the cold tea.

findings.line: Kel'Thuzad regarded her for a long moment.
findings.reason: Generic gaze beat interchangeable with any character. 'Regarded for a long moment' is stock.
findings.suggestedFix: Kel'Thuzad tapped one finger on the folio.

findings.line: Kel'Thuzad looked at her for a long time.
findings.reason: Second generic gaze beat — near-duplicate of L325. Stock duration marker.
findings.suggestedFix: Kel'Thuzad turned the chalk once between his fingers.

findings.line: She could feel the last weeks in her bones
findings.reason: Filter verb 'could feel' distances the reader. Go direct.
findings.suggestedFix: The last weeks pressed in her bones

findings.line: Kel'Thuzad seemed satisfied by that alone.
findings.reason: Hedge verb 'seemed' refuses commitment. Either he's satisfied or he isn't.
findings.suggestedFix: Kel'Thuzad settled back, satisfied by that alone.

findings.line: That changed him a little.
findings.reason: Hedge 'a little' undercuts the observation. The next sentence ('The last warmth went out of him') does the work.
findings.suggestedFix: That reached him.

findings.line: The cruelty of it lay in the fact that he was probably right there too.
findings.reason: Hedge 'probably' where the scene has already demonstrated he IS right.
findings.suggestedFix: The cruelty of it lay in the fact that he was right there too.

findings.line: The answer came down neat as a blade.
findings.reason: Narrator captioning how the answer landed. The dialogue already carries its own weight.

findings.line: It came out without humor.
findings.reason: Narrator translating the laugh. 'She laughed once' stands alone.

findings.line: The mention of her teacher hit low and deep.
findings.reason: Narrator charting the emotional trajectory. The dialogue that follows shows the impact.

findings.line: Two names now. Two shocks.
findings.reason: Narrator counting for the reader. We just heard both names.

findings.line: He had been waiting for comprehension since she entered the room.
findings.reason: Narrator retroactively explaining KT's agenda. His 'Yes' and her physical recoil carry the moment.

findings.line: The sanctum's banality became unbearable.
findings.reason: Thesis sentence opening an evidence paragraph. The inventory that follows should demonstrate banality without the label.

findings.line: There, stripped bare, was the real obscenity.
findings.reason: Thesis label before the explanation. 'A willingness to call cruelty by a clean name and keep building' does the work alone.

findings.line: He left room where the next inference belonged and her mind rushed to fill it.
findings.reason: Narrator explaining the Socratic technique. The preceding dialogue exchange already shows it in action.

findings.line: The words landed and stayed.
findings.reason: Generic closure caption. Impact is shown by Jaina turning away.

findings.line: And beyond them an older force using all of it as groundwork.
findings.reason: Closure extension. The inventory list (Plague villages. Grain roads. Scholomance.) already conveys scale.

findings.line: There it was.
findings.reason: Generic closure marker. 'The beauty of the structure' that follows does the work.

findings.line: The word broke the spell of it.
findings.reason: Narrator captioning the break. Jaina's physical jerk backward shows the spell breaking.

findings.line: The theorem had crossed into her and would stay there with everything else she had learned on this road that could never be made harmless again.
findings.reason: Closure expansion. 'Nothing would erase it now' in the preceding sentence already establishes permanence.

findings.line: She had the answer now.
findings.reason: Closure summary. The entire chapter has led here — the reader knows.

findings.line: Understanding the mechanism had cost her enough. Understanding that cost almost as much.
findings.reason: Closure epigram. Neat parallel structure = narrator capping the scene. The preceding paragraphs already show both costs.

findings.line: The sight of it told her the conversation had turned before he spoke.
findings.reason: Sight given communication verb 'told.' The diagram does not tell — Jaina reads it.
findings.suggestedFix: She saw the diagram and knew the conversation had turned before he spoke.

findings.line: the room widened around her
findings.reason: Room given physical behavior (widening). Jaina's perception, not the room's action.

findings.line: his calm had to work to cover appetite
findings.reason: Abstract quality (calm) given intentional labor verb (had to work). Calm does not labor.
findings.suggestedFix: his composure slipped and showed appetite beneath

findings.line: suddenly lack filled it
findings.reason: Absence (lack) given active verb (filled). Lack cannot fill — it is the absence of filling.
findings.suggestedFix: and the absence hit her all at once

findings.line: without letting the ground choose the pace
findings.reason: Ground given intention verb (choose). Ground does not choose — the person navigates.
findings.suggestedFix: and kept their own pace through it

findings.line: The building itself was telling her what kind of mind was in it now.
findings.reason: Building given communication verb (telling). Jaina reads the ward signatures; the building does not communicate.
findings.suggestedFix: She read the descent for what it was and knew what kind of mind had entered.

findings.line: The shambling choir.
findings.reason: Metaphor from liturgical/music domain. Jaina's register is academic/arcane — she would use KT's own terminology for the collective dead, not a choir metaphor that serves thematic irony over character voice.
findings.suggestedFix: The rough answerables.

findings.line: The mind that followed pattern had no built-in mercy. Mercy came from elsewhere or not at all.
findings.reason: Thesis-fragment pair. Generalizes from personal (her mind) to universal (the mind) — a narrator move. Could appear as an epigraph without modification.
findings.suggestedFix: Her mind ran toward pattern by default. Mercy required someone else in the room.

findings.line: Early enough to see all of it and read it wrong.
findings.reason: Thesis-fragment pair forming antithetical epigraph with preceding line ('Too late to stop the moment'). Narrator-grade compression despite in-character content.
findings.suggestedFix: But Sylvanas would see all of it and read it wrong.

clusterWarnings: Lines 625 and 641 repeat 'No X. No Y.' in same scene
KEEP at L613 downgraded to KILL by cluster rule (near 2 KILLs)

findings.line: No shame. No evasion. Only mild surprise that the point would need saying aloud.
findings.reason: Chained negation variant (No X. No Y. Only Z). Deletion test: 'Mild surprise that the point would need saying aloud' survives. Self-check 4: the shame/evasion distinction does not drive character action.
findings.suggestedFix: Mild surprise that the point would need saying aloud.

findings.line: The partnership had never been comfort. It had been correction.
findings.reason: Source-swap pivot. Deletion test: 'The partnership had been correction' survives and gains directness.
findings.suggestedFix: The partnership had been correction.

findings.line: Attention lifting, not alarmed, simply redirected.
findings.reason: Minimizer pivot. Initially passed condition 1 (binary diagnostic) but downgraded to KILL by cluster rule — appears near 2 KILLs at L625 and L641.
findings.suggestedFix: Attention lifting, redirected.

findings.line: No false starts. No panicked burst of speed where a turn narrowed.
findings.reason: Chained negation. Deletion test: surrounding text ('The building registering a descent that wasted nothing... Whoever was coming knew how to move...') survives. Same pattern shape repeats at L641.

findings.line: No wasted motion. No magical show.
findings.reason: Chained negation. Deletion test: surrounding text survives. Same pattern shape as L625.

findings.line: White dust marked her fingertips.
findings.reason: Third verb use of 'marked' in chapter (cap: 2). Weakest instance — repeats L31's chalk-dust observation with minimal new information.
findings.suggestedFix: White dust clung to her fingertips.

summary.verdictReason: 38 KILL verdicts across 5 guides. Top: gpt-5 F7 (8), gpt-5 F4 (5), negation Pattern 7 (3), intent-smear P5 (3). All 38 with suggested fixes (22 deletions, 16 rewrites). Severe clusters at lines 507-540 (comprehension shock) and lines 611-645 (approach section).
